video.title: How to Automate Construction Daily Reports (n8n + AI Step-by-Step)
video.angle: Automating progress photo uploads, daily summaries, and ClickUp safety tickets using n8n and AI
video.hook: Construction daily reports are critical, but chasing field workers for updates and manually organizing photos is a massive waste of time. In this video, I'm going to show you how to automate the entire process with n8n and AI.

production.style: strict follow-along tutorial with real UI anchors, live test runs, and no static filler

scenes.title: Automation Preview
scenes.voiceover: Construction daily reports are absolutely critical for project visibility, but chasing field workers for updates and manually organizing photos is a massive waste of time. In this step-by-step tutorial, I will show you how to build a complete automated pipeline that takes a simple mobile form submission, organizes progress photos in Google Drive, writes a clean operations summary using AI, and alerts the PM. Let's first inspect the finished result from a live run.
scenes.visual_note: A high-tech overview dashboard of the construction report automation showing Google Forms, n8n canvas, and Gmail.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open the Gmail inbox and verify the daily report summary.
demo_steps.expected_result: A beautifully formatted HTML report email is visible.
demo_steps.visual_anchor: Inbox daily summary email open on screen.

visual_plan.cursor_action: verify Gmail inbox daily summary email

scenes.title: ClickUp Safety Escalation
scenes.voiceover: If a field worker reports a safety hazard or incident in their submission, the automation instantly routes the details to ClickUp, creating a high-priority ticket in the Site Issues list. This ensures safety issues are addressed immediately by the safety officer and never forgotten on paper. You can download the complete template from the link in the description. Please subscribe and leave a comment to support this channel, and may God bless you.
scenes.visual_note: ClickUp dashboard showing high-priority safety ticket.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open the ClickUp dashboard and inspect the created task.
demo_steps.expected_result: A high-priority ticket titled 'Safety Hazard: Water near power cords' is visible.

scenes.title: Create n8n Workflow
scenes.voiceover: To start building, open your self-hosted n8n instance or cloud dashboard in your browser. Click the Create New Workflow button in the top right corner of the dashboard interface. This opens a blank canvas where we will build our automated pipeline step-by-step. Make sure to save your canvas frequently as we add nodes to prevent losing work. This will ensure your workflow runs locally or on your own VPS server without paying Zapier fees.
scenes.visual_note: n8n dashboard with a blank canvas open.

demo_steps.ui_target: n8n dashboard Create New Workflow button
demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open n8n dashboard and click the Create New Workflow button.
demo_steps.expected_result: A blank canvas with a start node is loaded.

visual_plan.cursor_action: click n8n dashboard Create New Workflow button

scenes.title: Configure Credentials
scenes.voiceover: Double-click the default workflow name at the top left of the canvas and enter 'Construction Daily Report Automation'. Next, open the active credentials settings panel. Ensure that your API accounts for Google Sheets, Google Drive, OpenAI GPT-4o, Gmail, and ClickUp are fully authenticated, connected, and displaying active status badges.
scenes.visual_note: Workflow rename prompt and API credentials status panel.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Enter 'Construction Daily Report Automation' into the workflow name field.
demo_steps.expected_result: The name updates in the header.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open the credentials page and select Google, OpenAI, Gmail, and ClickUp.
demo_steps.expected_result: Connected indicators show green status.

visual_plan.cursor_action: enter Workflow name input field; connect API credentials settings panel

scenes.title: Create Intake Form
scenes.voiceover: Open Google Forms in your browser and click the blank form template option. Name the form 'Construction Daily Report'. Add input fields for Project Name, Date, Inspector Name, Crew Size, Weather, Work Completed, Delays, Safety Issues, and a File Upload question named Photos, restricting it to images with a ten megabyte file limit. Ensure the fields are named logically to avoid data extraction confusion later.
scenes.visual_note: Google Forms editing page showing daily report questions.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Go to Google Forms and select blank form.
demo_steps.expected_result: A new empty form is loaded.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add questions for Project Name, Date, Crew Size, Weather, Delays, Safety, and Photos.
demo_steps.expected_result: Form layout has all required inputs.

visual_plan.cursor_action: open Google Forms editor; create Google Forms question fields

scenes.title: Link Sheet Database
scenes.voiceover: Once your intake form is designed, select the Responses tab at the top of the form editor. Click the green Link to Sheets button and choose Create a new spreadsheet. This creates a Google Sheet database that will record every form submission in real-time, acting as our central ledger.
scenes.visual_note: Google Sheet responding spreadsheet displaying form output fields.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open the Responses tab and click Link to Sheets.
demo_steps.expected_result: A spreadsheet setup modal appears.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Choose create new spreadsheet and select Create.
demo_steps.expected_result: A new Google Sheet is opened with matching headers.

visual_plan.cursor_action: click Google Forms Responses tab; create New spreadsheet output

scenes.title: Add Google Sheets Trigger
scenes.voiceover: Go back to n8n and drag the Google Sheets Trigger node onto the canvas. Set the event option to On Row Added. Connect your Google account and select the response sheet we created in the previous step, ensuring the sheet name matches exactly. Verify that the connection credentials show a green status dot before moving to the next node.
scenes.visual_note: n8n Google Sheets trigger node configuration sidebar panel.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Search for Google Sheets trigger and add it to canvas.
demo_steps.expected_result: The node appears on the canvas.

demo_steps.ui_target: On Row Added trigger event configuration
demo_steps.exact_instruction: Select event type On Row Added and select spreadsheet 'Construction Daily Report'.
demo_steps.expected_result: The node is configured and ready.
demo_steps.visual_anchor: On Row Added trigger event configuration.

foreground_elements: On Row Added trigger event configuration
visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n Sheets trigger node; set On Row Added trigger event configuration
visual_plan.on_screen_text: STEP 2: CONNECT n8n TO GOOGLE SHEETS

scenes.title: Test Inbound Payload
scenes.voiceover: Submit a test daily report using the Google Form. In n8n, click the Listen for test event button. The trigger node will fire and fetch the JSON response payload. Verify that all fields like Project Name and Weather appear correctly in the output panel.
scenes.visual_note: n8n canvas displaying inbound payload JSON output.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Submit a form report and click Listen for test event in n8n.
demo_steps.expected_result: Payload appears with fields like Project Name.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Check that form variables are mapped inside the JSON.
demo_steps.expected_result: Fields like Weather and Crew Size exist.

visual_plan.cursor_action: click Listen for test event button; verify Inbound payload JSON output
visual_plan.on_screen_text: STEP 3: RUN EVENT INTAKE SCHEMA TEST

scenes.title: Search Drive Folder
scenes.voiceover: Now we set up folder organization in Google Drive. Add a Google Drive node to check if a project folder matching the Project Name already exists. Configure the action to Get Folders and enter a search query query matching the folder name dynamically.
scenes.visual_note: n8n Google Drive search query configuration panel.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Search for Google Drive node and add it to canvas.
demo_steps.expected_result: The node appears on the canvas.

demo_steps.ui_target: Google Drive Get Folders action config
demo_steps.exact_instruction: Select Action 'Get Folders' and set query to match form 'Project Name'.
demo_steps.expected_result: The lookup query is saved.
demo_steps.visual_anchor: Google Drive Get Folders action config.

foreground_elements: Google Drive Get Folders action config
visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n Google Drive node; set Google Drive Get Folders action config

scenes.title: Create Project Folder
scenes.voiceover: Add an If node to check if the folder ID exists. Connect the False path to a Google Drive Create Folder node. Set the folder name to the Project Name from the Form. This dynamically creates a folder for new projects, keeping files organized.
scenes.visual_note: n8n If node branching logic for folder check.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add an If node to check if folder lookup returned empty.
demo_steps.expected_result: If node is placed after Google Drive search node.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add a Google Drive node to False branch and select Create Folder action.
demo_steps.expected_result: Folder creation node is connected.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n If node folder check; add Google Drive Create Folder node

scenes.title: Download Photo Binary
scenes.voiceover: To save progress photos, we must download the files first. Add an HTTP Request node to get the image URL from the Sheets trigger. Set the response format to file to download the raw binary image data into n8n.
scenes.visual_note: n8n HTTP Request node binary download settings.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add HTTP Request node to download file.
demo_steps.expected_result: HTTP Request node is connected.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Set Action to GET, map URL, and select Response Format to 'File'.
demo_steps.expected_result: Node is set to pull file binary data.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n HTTP Request node; set HTTP Request Response Format setting

scenes.title: Upload Photos to Drive
scenes.voiceover: Add a Google Drive Upload File node. Map the binary data input from the HTTP node. Set the Parent Folder ID to the project folder ID we looked up. This saves all site photos in the correct project folder automatically.
scenes.visual_note: n8n Google Drive upload node file properties mapping panel.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add Google Drive node and select Upload File action.
demo_steps.expected_result: Upload node is connected.

demo_steps.ui_target: Upload parent folder and binary parameter
demo_steps.exact_instruction: Map Parent Folder ID and set Binary Property to 'data'.
demo_steps.expected_result: Drive upload node is configured.
demo_steps.visual_anchor: Upload parent folder and binary parameter.

foreground_elements: Upload parent folder and binary parameter
visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n Google Drive upload node; map Upload parent folder and binary parameter
visual_plan.on_screen_text: STEP 4: UPLOAD PHOTOS TO PROJECT FOLDER

scenes.title: Add OpenAI Node
scenes.voiceover: Next, drag the OpenAI Chat node onto the canvas. Connect it after the folder upload step. Choose model gpt-4o from the dropdown menu. We will use this node to translate raw crew notes into a polished executive report.
scenes.visual_note: n8n OpenAI node connection and model selection setup.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Search for OpenAI Chat node and add it to canvas.
demo_steps.expected_result: The node appears on the canvas.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Select GPT-4o from the model options.
demo_steps.expected_result: The model is loaded.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n OpenAI Chat node; select OpenAI Model selection dropdown menu

scenes.title: Write AI Prompt
scenes.voiceover: Open the prompt settings in the OpenAI node. Paste our structured operations prompt template. Map the dynamic variables from the Google Sheets trigger node directly into the prompt text to provide the raw daily report data to the AI model.
scenes.visual_note: n8n OpenAI prompt editor showing report summary rules and JSON fields.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Open Prompt settings and paste the daily report summary prompt.
demo_steps.expected_result: The prompt template is active.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Drag dynamic variables for Weather, Work Completed, and Crew Size into prompt.
demo_steps.expected_result: JSON fields are mapped dynamically.

visual_plan.cursor_action: enter OpenAI prompt editor text box; map Prompt dynamic variables

scenes.title: Evaluate Safety Issues
scenes.voiceover: Add an If node after the OpenAI step. Set the condition to check if the 'Safety Issues' field is empty. If it contains text, the workflow routes to the True branch; otherwise, it skips task creation and goes straight to email.
scenes.visual_note: n8n If node check for safety issues text content.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add If node and name it Check Safety.
demo_steps.expected_result: If node is placed after OpenAI node.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Set value to check if Safety Issues string is not empty.
demo_steps.expected_result: The conditional rule is active.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n If node check safety; set If node condition logic selector

scenes.title: Create ClickUp Task
scenes.voiceover: Connect the True branch of the If node to a ClickUp Create Task node. Set list ID to Site Issues and priority to High. Map the task name and description to show the reporter's name and the safety issue details.
scenes.visual_note: n8n ClickUp node configuration panel showing list ID and high priority.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add ClickUp node to True path of If node.
demo_steps.expected_result: ClickUp node is connected.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Select Action Create Task, list 'Site Issues', priority 'High', and map name.
demo_steps.expected_result: ClickUp task fields are configured.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n ClickUp node; map ClickUp task parameters list priority

scenes.title: Gmail Delivery Setup
scenes.voiceover: Finally, add a Gmail Send Email node to notify the PM. Set the body type to HTML. Paste the styled HTML code template, mapping the AI summary and the Google Drive folder link dynamically so the PM has access.
scenes.visual_note: n8n Gmail node configuration panel with HTML body template.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Add Gmail node after ClickUp node, merging paths.
demo_steps.expected_result: Gmail node is connected.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Select Send Email, set recipient, choose HTML body, and map AI output.
demo_steps.expected_result: Gmail delivery settings are saved.

visual_plan.cursor_action: add n8n Gmail node; map Gmail HTML email body field

scenes.title: Turn Workflow Live
scenes.voiceover: Click the active toggle switch in the top right to turn the workflow live. Submit a test daily report to verify that n8n runs green, ClickUp receives the task, and Gmail receives the report. May God bless you. This setup will completely eliminate manual report chasing and keep your operations structured.
scenes.visual_note: n8n active toggle glowing green on n8n canvas header.

demo_steps.exact_instruction: Click the active toggle in the top right.
demo_steps.expected_result: The workflow is active and listening.

visual_plan.cursor_action: click n8n active workflow toggle switch
visual_plan.on_screen_text: STEP 8: RUN FULL SYSTEM END-TO-END TEST

metadata.description_template:
Construction daily reports are critical, but chasing field workers for updates and manually organizing photos is a massive waste of time. In this video, I'm going to show you how to automate the entire process with n8n and AI.

💬 AUTOMATION CHALLENGE:
Describe your B2B daily operational tasks in the comments, and I will write a custom workflow guide to automate it.

📋 CHAPTERS:
0:00 — Hook
1:30 — Workspace Setup
3:00 — Intake Form Setup
4:30 — Sheets Trigger
5:45 — Google Drive folders
7:45 — OpenAI summarizer
8:45 — ClickUp safety tickets
9:45 — Gmail notifications
10:15 — Live end-to-end test

📥 **Download the complete workflow blueprints:**
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1NB4deu0_7g23VByy4txQXYjtjJONZcrD

#AIAutomation #B2B #ClaudeAI #SaaS #Productivity
metadata.pinned_comment_template:
🤖 Pinned by AI Operator
Did you get stuck setting up n8n binary downloads from Google Forms? Let me know in the comments and I'll jump in to help! Also, let me know what B2B stack you want to see automated next.
metadata.thumbnail_concept: Sleek dark background (#0d1117). Mobile mockup of daily form with hard hat on left; n8n workflow canvas glowing green on right. Text overlay: 'AUTOMATE DAILY REPORTS'
